name: Organometallic Catalytic Cycle
description: Homogeneous organometallic catalysis turns over through elementary organometallic steps - substrate coordination, oxidative addition, migratory insertion, and reductive elimination - with the 16/18-electron rule governing whether a vacant site is available for the next step.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Precatalyst complex"]
    N2["Generate active species"]
    N3["Count electrons (16/18)"]
    N4["Substrate coordinates"]
    N5["Oxidative addition"]
    N6["Migratory insertion"]
    N7{"18-electron saturated?"}
    N8["Ligand dissociates"]
    N9["Reductive elimination"]
    N10["Regenerate catalyst"]
    N11["Catalytic turnover"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 -->|yes| N8
    N8 -->|reopen| N4
    N7 -->|no| N9
    N9 --> N10
    N10 -->|next turn| N3
    N9 --> N11

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: The 16 and 18 electron rule in organometallic chemistry and homogeneous catalysis
The Organometallic Chemistry of the Transition Metals, 6th ed.
Inorganic Chemistry, 6th ed. — Organometallic Catalysis
sources.authors: Housecroft, C. E.; Sharpe, A. G.

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.
